name: ls
description: List directory contents.

name: cd
description: Change the current working directory.

name: pwd
description: Print the full path of the current working directory.

name: mkdir
description: Create one or more directories.

name: rm
description: Remove files or directories.

name: cp
description: Copy files or directories.

name: mv
description: Move or rename files and directories.

name: cat
description: Concatenate and display file contents.

name: grep
description: Search for patterns in files or input.

name: find
description: Search for files and directories in a directory hierarchy.

name: chmod
description: Change file or directory permissions.

name: chown
description: Change file owner and group.

name: echo
description: Display a line of text or write to a file.

name: man
description: Display the manual page for a command.

name: ssh
description: Open a secure shell connection to a remote host.

name: scp
description: Securely copy files between hosts over SSH.

name: curl
description: Transfer data from or to a server using various protocols.

name: wget
description: Download files from the web.

name: tar
description: Archive and compress files.

name: zip
description: Package and compress files into a zip archive.

name: unzip
description: Extract files from a zip archive.

name: ps
description: Display information about running processes.

name: kill
description: Send a signal to a process, typically to terminate it.

name: top
description: Display real-time system resource usage and running processes.

name: htop
description: Interactive process viewer (enhanced version of top).

name: df
description: Report disk space usage of file systems.

name: du
description: Estimate file and directory disk usage.

name: ping
description: Check network connectivity to a host.

name: ifconfig
description: Configure or display network interface parameters.

name: ip
description: Show and manipulate routing, network devices, and tunnels.

name: netstat
description: Print network connections, routing tables, and interface statistics.

name: history
description: Display or manage the command history list.

name: alias
description: Create a shortcut for a command.

name: env
description: Display, set, or remove environment variables.

name: export
description: Set environment variables for child processes.

name: source
description: Execute commands from a file in the current shell.

name: which
description: Show the full path of a shell command.

name: whereis
description: Locate the binary, source, and manual page files for a command.

name: wc
description: Count lines, words, and characters in files.

name: head
description: Output the first lines of a file.

name: tail
description: Output the last lines of a file.

name: sort
description: Sort lines of text.

name: uniq
description: Report or filter out repeated lines.

name: sed
description: Stream editor for filtering and transforming text.

name: awk
description: Pattern scanning and text processing language.

name: diff
description: Compare files line by line.

name: ln
description: Create hard or symbolic links.

name: touch
description: Create an empty file or update file timestamps.

name: crontab
description: Schedule commands to run automatically at specified times.

name: sudo
description: Execute a command as another user (typically root).

name: apt
description: Package management utility for Debian-based systems.
examples: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade

name: git
description: Distributed version control system.
